summary: Introducing solid foods including cereals before 6 months increases the risk of developing food allergies; however, no substantial data indicates such an association directly causes severe gastrointestinal issues beyond typical digestive discomforts common during infancy transition periods into new diets. Following delayed introduction aligns better with current medical recommendations aimed primarily at reducing allergen sensitivity risks rather than preventing general gut health complications arising solely from timing alone without other contributing factors present simultaneously.

Answer choice: A
